notes: Solo curation (no upstream PR; legitimate user-driven task). Test/patch alignment is precise: canonical patch adds cache_results=True to exactly 5 @triton.autotune sites in 3 files (linear.py: kernel_fwd, kernel_bwd; layer_norm.py: _layer_norm_fwd_1pass_kernel, _layer_norm_bwd_kernel; flash_attn_triton.py: _bwd_kernel) and the test.sh F2P gates are the same 5 sites, weight 0.18 each + 0.10 completeness = 1.00 sum. Verifier uses mock-exec of the decorator AST (not regex match), so it accepts any formatting/ordering of cache_results=True and verifies key list isn't destructively emptied — solid behavioral verification, not over-fit. The 5 reconstruction warnings on AMD-only files (flash_attn_triton_amd/fwd_prefill.py, fwd_decode.py, bwd.py) are harmless: those files don't exist in the base repo (Dao-AILab/flash-attention at ef736fe), the session tried to patch them but the tool replay failed, and the verifier deliberately does NOT test them — it scopes to the 5 sites that actually exist and got patched. P2P gates are declared kind=P2P_GATING with weight 0.0 (correctly informational/structural-floor: syntax + kernel-name preservation), enforced as hard zero in test.sh — no P2P_REGRESSION zero-reward bug pattern. No instruction leakage (instruction is the verbatim 1-line user message: 'Add cache_results to torch.autotune in this repo'). Dockerfile pins triton==3.6.0 (the version that has cache_results; earlier 3.2.0 made the task unsolvable per fix_summary.md). Healthy discrimination: Sonnet 4.6 = 0.90 vs Haiku 4.5 = 0.67 (gap 0.23). KEEP.
